body_txt: Talk aboot the trope here, eh? In American media, Canada is a sweet, quirky and slightly backwards version of America . It's as if you took everyone from Minnesota , gave them an obsession with hockey note Anyone who thinks this should be clarified to Ice Hockey will be stiffly beaten by the RCMP, with a hockey stick. (OK, it's practically an unofficial religion), and made that an entire country. Everybody's white (except the First Nations), note ignoring the over 1 in 4 who are neither and if someone's last name isn't French, then they have a Scottish note or English, Welsh or Irish name. note And sometimes both; it's not rare to find 'Pierre MacDonald ' or 'Iain Beaurepaire' in Canada. People with German ancestry also make up almost 10 percent of Canada's total population, making them one of the larger ethnic groups in Canada. Eeeeeh, Canadians eat nothing but Kraft Dinner (which is Canadian for "macaroni and cheese"), Tim Hortons coffee, doughnuts, note and doughnut holes AKA Timbits poutine, note French fries with cheese curds, and enough gravy to partially melt the cheese and Canadian... er... peameal bacon (American "Canadian bacon" is actually completely different from peameal and is not actually popular in Canada). Anglophone Canadians all speak with a stereotyped West/Central Canadian English accent, putting "eh" at the end of questions or affirmations , note although New Zealanders say "eh" more often than even the most stereotypical Canadian , but again, not many people outside both New Zealand and the Land Down Under know this and prominently raising the "ou" in aboot every word containing it. Also, as per the page image, Canadians are famed for shamelessly holding their maple syrup jugs on the non-handle side . note though, if you look closely the left jug in the image has two handles The army consists of a guy with a BB gun mounted on a moose, the Royal Canadian Air Force a paper airplane, note and the Avro Arrow the coast guard a guy with a pair of binoculars and a rope, and the navy a guy in a canoe with a slingshot. note Americans military personnel who have served with Canadians throw this trope around for jokes, but in reality, tend to see Canadians as a professional force. In fact, the Canadian army has been quite aggressive in certain wars. All policemen are Mounties , and they wear their 19th-century-style red serge dress tunics and broad-brimmed Montana Peak hats constantly, even while on regular duty. Canadian technology is always behind American tech. note Except of course, Canadarm, ATI, Corel Wordperfect, the telephone and Alexander Graham Bell himself, the Blackberry, the Learjet, IMAX theaters, Toon Boom , wheelchair accessible buses, electric wheelchairs, snowmobiles, snowblowers, egg cartons, gas masks and the aforementioned Arrow, but who's counting, eh? In fact, if it wasn't for the Americans, they'd have no culture at all. Canadians feel so dominated by US TV, films and music, that the Canadian government funds the production and broadcasting of Canadian content. As well, TV stations and radio stations have to broadcast a minimum amount of Canadian content. The country is also likely to be always covered in snow regardless what time of year it is . Feel free to whack us Canucks over the head with a hockey stick . And doon't feel soarry aboot it, eh! We'll just apologize to you for possibly damaging your hockey stick, because that's the Canadian way, eh? We Canadians have a great capacity to laugh at ourselves and not take ourselves too seriously. In fact, we have the largest international comedy festival in the world and its associated long-running Candid Camera Prank show, Just for Laughs: Gags , is internationally syndicated in over 150 countries. Oh, but remember: No matter how polite and well-mannered Canada seems to be, there are always some exceptions. To say nothing of Canada geese, which look majestic but are basically just flying shitting miniature velociraptors . Be afraid. Useful Notes about non-fictional Canada now has their own page. Also see Canada Does Not Exist , Canadian Equals Hockey Fan , Courteous Canadian , and Minnesota Nice for the American equivalent. And Crass Canuck for the uncouth Canadian. Because this trope deals with national stereotypes, No Real Life Examples, Please!
